what_it_does: This file is an open noticeboard issue that records the verified state of the jina-v3 reranker being disabled on the decision grounding path, documents why it was removed, lists four loadout options, flags a related candidate-output normalization defect, and routes the choice as a tracked decision awaiting Tim.
observations: TIM-FLAGGED on 2026-06-18 because a 'fix later' removal had no tracked owner.
The decision recall path in runtime/territory.py:267 passes rerank=False, so the jina reranker is functionally off for decisions.
jina-v3 is CPU-only on port :8008, causing roughly 1 s per candidate and about 24 s total, which exceeded the latency budget.
The reranker is still used by the CC consult path, which pays about 10 s.
The only live decision caller passes rerank=False; the 'stays available for unbounded paths' claim has no live decision caller.
The 'fix later' note lives only in build-prep/the-one-application/RERANK-CPU-PERF.md and a code comment, with no board item or owner.
Four options are listed: keep rerank off latency-bounded paths, move jina-v3 to GPU, add a lighter CPU cross-encoder, or shrink/async the candidate set.
A side finding notes that 13 of 24 pooled candidates have a non-string output shape, causing rerank_hits to silently skip them.
The owner/next step is explicitly a Tim loadout decision, to be rendered as a decision card.
standouts: For decisions, the reranker is functionally removed because every live caller passes rerank=False.
The issue was previously untracked despite being a loadout/architecture decision, not a unilateral code change.
A separate grounding-quality bug is embedded: non-string outputs silently bypass rerank_hits.
The issue frames the rerank on/off choice as a decision-set card that should be decided on the decision surface.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history.
Follows the noticeboard item format with board:// address and source claude_code.
Body is Markdown with section headers and bullet lists.
References code locations and file paths explicitly.
Uses a TIM-FLAGGED prefix and a named owner/decision-maker.
concerns: State is open but there is no concrete assignee or due date beyond 'Tim loadout decision'.
A separate defect (non-string output shapes) is bundled as a sub-item rather than filed as its own tracked issue.
Some claims about code paths and timings are asserted as verified but not reproducible from this file alone.
Phrases like 'DNA renders it through the wire when B lands' are metaphorical and not operationally defined here.
notes: Tracked open issue that turns the disabled decision-path reranker into an explicit Tim loadout decision with four options, plus a side note about non-string outputs silently breaking reranking.
questions: Who is 'Tim' and what is the formal loadout decision process?
What do 'RHM', 'CC consult path', and 'DNA renders it through the wire when B lands' refer to?
Have the GPU VRAM cost and exact consult-path latency been measured?
Is the 13/24 non-string output issue filed as a separate item?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to make the reranker removal a tracked, decided decision; the file does that by documenting the state, options, and owner, so they align.
apparent_themes: Silent functional removal of a component
Data-shape normalization as a quality bug
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent an unowned 'fix later' from being forgotten by promoting it to a visible decision card with explicit options and a named decision-maker, while also surfacing a related corpus-capture defect.
novelty: It treats a code-level toggle (rerank=False) as a first-class decision-set item and notes that the decision surface should decide the rerank decision itself.
contribution: It contributes a durable, traceable record of a latent architectural choice, a structured set of loadout options, and a side observation about output-shape normalization, all usable as first-class inputs to downstream decision queries.
summary_for_embedding: Open noticeboard issue item-a3844c46 records that the jina-v3 reranker is functionally disabled on the decision grounding path via rerank=False, lists four loadout options awaiting a Tim decision, and notes a separate corpus bug where non-string candidate outputs silently skip reranking.
intention_for_embedding: To make the previously untracked removal of the decision-path reranker into an explicit, owned decision item and to flag a related candidate-output normalization defect.
